items.title: Federalism, Nationalism and Development: India and the Punjab Economy
items.notes: Special Indian Reprint 2009, Second edition 2019. Brings together groundbreaking methodological approaches and field level data to add new dimensions to the understanding of federal modes of governance and the development process, particularly in India. 'The book is one of those rare academic publications which have the potential to make history' (Economic and Political Weekly)

items.title: Economy, Culture and Human Rights: Turbulence in Punjab, India and Beyond
items.notes: Marshals diverse theory and evidence drawn from economics, political science, moral philosophy, and history to make a case for understanding human rights as intrinsically important rather than simply as a means to achieve economic and political goals. Subject of extended TV interview on Sky/Sikh Channel

items.title: Punjabi Identity in a Global Context
items.authors: Pritam Singh and Shinder Singh Thandi (eds)
items.notes: Reprint 2015. Co-edited pioneering volume addressing Punjabi identity in diaspora and multicultural contexts Second edition published 2015 by Oxford University Press.

items.title: Hindu Bias in Indian Constitution
items.notes: Examines constitutional flaws and institutional communalism in India's governance framework

items.title: Institutional Communalism in India
items.notes: Analysis of institutional communalism and its implications for governance and rights in India

items.title: Equal Opportunities in the Curriculum
items.notes: Co-edited pioneering work on pedagogic research, exploring equal opportunities in teaching concerning curriculum, teaching methodologies and assessment

items.title: Globalisation and the Region: Explorations in Punjabi Identity
items.authors: Pritam Singh and Shinder Singh Thandi (eds)
items.notes: Equal contribution of the editors. A revised and updated version was published as 'Punjabi Identity in a Global Context' (Oxford University Press, 1999 and 2015).

items.title: Women and Society: Problems and Prospects
